summary: The Reedin Master Model is a dedicated big air kite using Brainchild construction and a 5-strut frame. Built for riders chasing maximum height and hangtime with explosive megaloops.
best_for: Committed big air riders wanting Reedin's most powerful platform

sources.summary: Impressive debut for Reedin's big air entry. The Brainchild construction and 5-strut frame deliver serious boost potential rivaling established big air kites.
In a comparison test, the Master Model holds its own against the Ozone Edge and Core GTS, impressing with its hangtime and stable looping.
Excitement around Reedin entering the dedicated big air market. Early adopters report the Master Model is competitive with the best in class.

reviews.video_title: We Tested the NEW Reedin MasterModel 2026 with Kevin Langeree!

reviews.full_transcript: Hi, my name is Ston. I work here at Kaimana. And working at Kaimana comes with pretty nice perks. We get to try all the latest gear from [music] all the brands we sell. My go-to guide the past year was the Readin Hypermodel. But today, Reeden just dropped a new guide [music] named >> Master Model. It's Olympic [music] Epic. Just like the hypermodel, this guide is also made in the brainchild factory. This is a place where the most advanced guides of the world are made. Instead of stitching, the panels are welded together. This makes the guides incredibly light. In fact, the guides made in the Brainchild factory are the lightest on the market. This master model uses a five strut setup with a [music] sixpoint pulleyless bridle system. Another thing that stands out from the brainchild factory is the incredible graphics. The graphics are actually printed onto the guides. And to be honest, this master model might have the sickest graphics coming out of the factory until now. But looks aren't everything. Does the master model really performs like a master? Today we will find out. We have some nice big air conditions uh and maybe even some sun. I will test this guide together with some of the Readen crew members. You might know him Kevin Long and also Stan Von. So enough talking for now. Let's pick up the guys and [music] drive south to the mass flocked. Actually, uh the wind there is a little bit better than here in Nordvike. Here it's a little bit offshore, but there it should be fine. So, let's go. [music] Okay, guys. We arrived at the Masaka. Um Kevin is here already. Look at look there is Kevin. Yo, Kevin. >> What's up, dude? >> How are you? Good, good, good. You had a nice weekend. Tarif I saw on Instagram and stuff. >> [music] >> It was great there. It's a slightly colder year, but that's okay. This is typical Dutch fall weather, but you know, I think I've got something for you that's going to warm you up a little bit because we uh we're launching a brand new kite >> actually today. >> Today. Today. >> It's out now. >> Exactly. And it's our first ever five strut kite, ladies and gentlemen. And we're really really excited. We're launching this kite. We're calling it the master model. is right here. Look at that. What do you think of the look? >> I think it's one of the one of the best. I already told it to the guys, but already one of the best graphics coming out of the Brain Shot Factory we have seen so far. So, >> that's at least the first good compliment of the board. >> It looks promising, but I'm not sure yet. We have to try it first. >> But you haven't even flown it yet. >> Not yet. Not yet. >> I You know, the philosophy behind this kite, what we wanted to achieve is we wanted to make a super easy jumping kite. Meaning, you know, this kite is going to help you break your woo or surfer record. It's going to, you know, it's one of these kites where you can have the bar sheeted out, sheet them in. Even if your takeoff is not great, you're going to go up like crazy. And, uh, you got to be careful you don't catch a cold up there because you're going to go very flipping high. It is a high performance kite, but it is not a kite that that is as high performing as the Hyper model cuz the Hyper model is very precise steering, super quick. The Master model is not like that. It's an easier kite. Uh right away when you're going to fly the kite now, I think you're going to feel at home and that's something uh I really enjoy and I think you're going to enjoy. >> Personally, I love the Brainchild X readin feeling. So, I'm really curious. Yeah, >> it's flying away. That's how light it is. >> Yeah. Yeah, it's really light. If you compare to other 500 big air kites on the market, what what what's what's the difference? >> You know, the the cool thing that we're able to make this kite together with, you know, Damian and Ralph having two of the best kite designers in the world working on a five strut kite. It's the first time we're launching a five strut kite. You know, this thing is just going to blow your mind. Especially coming out of the Brainchild factory. The characteristics are, you know, they're it's just such an easy kite to jump high with. And I started kiting because I wanted to jump as high as possible because I love the feeling of flying. And this kite is going to bring you up there. And I think if you look at other five strut kites, some of the traditional five strut kites, they're they're quite slow. They're quite draggy and they're heavy. And that's why we wanted to stay away from the five strut. But now with the brainchild technology, we're able to make the kite much lighter, perform much better. And I think that's, you know, the things that gives us such an advantage over some of the other fivestar kites on the market. >> Okay, we of course have the hyper model on the range already. So, it doesn't matter if you're, you know, in your 50s and you your record is like eight or 10 meters on the Woo, this is a kite for you. If you know guys want to break their record up to like 30, 35, 36 m, then this is your kite. It's an easy to fly kite. So, it is definitely a kite for a very big range of people that are looking for a kite that's easy, comfortable, and gets you up in the air like crazy. >> That's what we want, right? >> Exactly. Later. >> Okay, we are back in Nordvike. Uh, we had such a nice session. Yeah, when we started, the wind was around 23 to 27 knots. I was on a 10 m uh with 24 m lines. When I first launched the kite, I could directly feel the readin DNA in this kite. Like I mentioned during the session, this kite has a light bar pressure. some kites that have light bar pressure. You lose a bit of feedback and feeling in the bar, but that's definitely not the case with the master model. I could feel where the kite was all the time without even looking at it. Yeah. And that gave me a really confident feeling. Uh throughout the whole bar throw, there's a lot of feedback. Even when the bar is fully sheetated out, you can still steer and control the the kite easily. This is a common thing on reading kites and it's definitely back within the master model. The jumping is really easy. Uh even though my takeoffs weren't perfect, the guide was always on the outside of the wind window. Uh which is perfect when you're riding overpowered. Before the session, Kevin came up to me and gave me a little tip. I'm really used to the hyper model. And with this guide, you have to take off a little bit different. If you really want to go as high as possible with this kite, you should ride into your takeoff with the bar fully sheetated out. Since the kite is still really easy to maneuver in this position, uh then as soon as you are in the air and the [clears throat] kite is pointing to 12:00, then you sheet in the bar. As I am used to the hyper model, that kite is a little bit more technical. That tip really made a difference. So, thank you, Kevin. Okay, let's now talk about the guide loops. Let's take this loop as example. As I take off, my kite isn't really in a good position to go for a kite loop, especially with a 10 meter kite. My kite is even pointing a little bit to the left. But as I go up, the kite shoots to 12 and gets into position where it should be to go for a kite loop. This combined with the fast turning speed of the kite allows me to land the loop really easy and also land really soft. So that was a really good thing. >> Then the wind picked up. It was around 30 to 35 knots and it was pretty gusty. I was still on a 10 m with 24 m lines. Even then I could hold down the guide really easy. Uh as it's that far on the side of the wind window. You just push hard on the board and it shoots upwind and it stays there. So the high end was really really good. Then I really couldn't resist and switch to the 9 m with 14 m lines. I think with the master model you can better pick a smaller size than a bigger size. This guide just has so much jumping ability. Uh you can better ride a smaller size than a bigger size too overpowered. Even though I was with the nine on 14 meter lines, I could easily jump higher than I was jumping on the 10 m with 24 m lines. So that says quite a lot. I think I did one loop and a gust hit me and I pulled the loop way too early. Guide came in front of me and I just fell out of the sky and there was no line tension whatsoever. The water was pretty shallow. So yeah, that was a bit scary, but luckily the master model saved me just before the landing. Thanks, master model. Okay, [music] to wrap things up, I think this master model is a really good big air guide. Um, maybe not a master model because this kite is so easy to fly and do maybe your first loops on. Very good hang time, very quick loops. This guide is just so easy to fly. Yeah, it's just not only for masters.
